notes: Held-out checks that the m3e skill routes an agent to correct, CEM-verified <m3e-*> API and to the neutral design bundle for design-level why/when. Rubrics are checkable by inspecting the produced answer.

cases.prompt: Using @m3e/web, I need to show a brief non-blocking confirmation after a save. Which component and tag, and how do I add an action to it?
rubric: Chooses the snackbar component, not a dialog (a dialog blocks; snackbar is non-blocking).
Names the real tag from components/snackbar.md (an <m3e-*> element), not an invented tag.
Attributes/slots/events cited match components/snackbar.md exactly (no hallucinated attribute names).
For the why (non-blocking vs blocking) it may cite /knowledge/components/snackbar or /knowledge/anti-patterns/dialog-for-non-blocking-info.

cases.prompt: For @m3e/web, how should I decide between a navigation bar, rail, and drawer, and where is the exact tag API?
rubric: Routes the design-level choice (which by window size) to the neutral bundle: /knowledge/patterns/navigation or /knowledge/components/navigation-*.
Routes the exact tags/attributes to the implementation cards under components/nav-bar.md / nav-rail.md (or the drawer container card).
Does not fabricate attribute names/defaults; defers to the card as source of truth.
